title: Satkosia Tiger Reserve
tagline: Satkosia Tiger Reserve — wildlife reserve of Odisha

overview.short: Satkosia Tiger Reserve is a tiger reserve located in the Angul and Nayagarh district of Odisha, India covering an area of 963.87 km2.
overview.description: Satkosia Tiger Reserve is a tiger reserve located in the Angul and Nayagarh district of Odisha, India covering an area of 963.87 km2. It was declared a wildlife sanctuary in 1976 and a tiger reserve in 2007.

topPlaces.name: Satkosia Gorge
topPlaces.description: Satkosia Gorge is a gorge in eastern Odisha, India, carved by the Mahanadi River. The gorge is located within the Satkosia Tiger Reserve which is a United nations Protected area.

items.note: Satkosia Gorge is a gorge in eastern Odisha, India, carved by the Mahanadi River. The gorge is located within the Satkosia Tiger Reserve which is a United nations Protected area.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Satkosia Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Satkosia Tiger Reserve is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Satkosia Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (108 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (109 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Satkosia Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Satkosia Tiger Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Satkosia Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Satkosia Tiger Reserve start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Satkosia Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are Satkosia Gorge.
